name: SimPy (Discrete-Event Simulation)

performance.notes: Pure Python generator-based; fast for DES; Cython version available

agent_readiness.notes: Discrete-event simulation using Python generators. import simpy. def process(env): yield env.timeout(5); print(f'Process at {env.now}'). env = simpy.Environment(). env.process(process(env)). env.run(until=10). Resources: server = simpy.Resource(env, capacity=1); with server.request() as req: yield req. Stores: simpy.Store(env). Container: simpy.Container(env, capacity=1000). Real-time: simpy.RealtimeEnvironment. Monitor: with server.request() as req: yield req | env.timeout(3). Queuing systems, manufacturing, supply chains.

gotchas: Generator-based coroutines can be confusing — yield env.timeout() is the core pattern
No built-in visualization — pair with matplotlib/plotly for output
No built-in statistics — compute manually from logged events
Large simulations with many processes can be slow in pure Python
best_when: Manufacturing process simulation and capacity planning
Healthcare systems modeling (patient flow, bed capacity)
avoid_when: Agent-based models with spatial interactions (use Mesa)
When high-performance simulation is needed (use custom C++)
summary: SimPy provides discrete-event simulation using Python generators. simpy.Environment(). process(env) with yield env.timeout(). Resource, Store, Container primitives. RealtimeEnvironment. MIT. Best for queuing systems, manufacturing simulation, and resource contention models. Generator pattern is core — yield env.timeout() and yield req. No built-in visualization or statistics.
